task_completion.description: Log into the OpenCAD system as an admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenCAD dashboard is visible with indicators of an active session for the 'admin' user (e.g., admin username in the top navigation or access to admin-only menus).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Incident Types management section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The admin settings or management page specifically for 'Incident Types' or 'Call Types' is visible in the OpenCAD interface.

task_completion.description: Add a new Incident Type named 'Rock Slide'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The string 'Rock Slide' is visible as a saved entry in the list of Incident Types, or a success notification confirms the creation of the 'Rock Slide' incident type.

task_completion.description: Open the form to create a new dispatch call
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'New Call', 'Create Dispatch', or active call creation form is open and visible in the OpenCAD interface.

task_completion.description: Create a new active call with the type 'Rock Slide' at 'North Haul Road'
task_completion.visual_evidence: An active call is visible on the dispatch board, active calls list, or call details screen, explicitly showing the Incident Type as 'Rock Slide' and the Location as 'North Haul Road'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenCAD web interface rather than using terminal scripts or database commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the OpenCAD application, with visible mouse clicks or form inputs, rather than a terminal window executing SQL or curl commands.

integrity.description: The agent actually submitted the new call and incident type, rather than just typing them into a form without saving.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Rock Slide' incident type and the active call appear in saved lists, tables, or dispatch boards, not just as unsubmitted text inside input fields.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the expected output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) pane is not open, and the UI styling looks consistent with the standard OpenCAD interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OpenCAD is a web-based Computer Aided Dispatch system. The task requires the agent to create a custom Incident Type named 'Rock Slide' and a dispatch call at 'North Haul Road'. Both are synthetic values. Verify that 'Rock Slide' appears in the system's Incident Types list and that an active call is successfully created displaying both 'Rock Slide' as the type and 'North Haul Road' as the location on the dispatch board.
